name: Paris, France
description: The city of lights, famous for art, architecture, romantic boulevards, and world-class cuisine.
transportation: Metro, RER trains, buses, bike rentals (Vélib'), river boats (Bateaux Parisiens)
safetyInfo: Generally safe. Watch for pickpockets in tourist areas, on the Metro, and near the Eiffel Tower. Keep belongings close.
photographySpots: Eiffel Tower at sunset from Trocadéro
travelTips: Buy museum passes in advance to skip lines
Learn basic French phrases — locals appreciate the effort
Use contactless payment widely accepted
Visit major attractions early morning
Validate your Metro ticket before boarding

name: Dubai, UAE
description: A modern metropolis known for luxury shopping, futuristic skyscrapers, and thrilling desert adventures.
transportation: Metro, taxis, ride-hailing apps (Careem, Uber), water taxis (Abra)
safetyInfo: Very safe city. Respect local customs, dress modestly in public, and avoid public displays of affection. Alcohol is permitted only in licensed venues.
travelTips: Stay hydrated — carry water at all times
Plan desert trips for early morning
Book Burj Khalifa tickets online to avoid queues
Use Nol card for Metro
Avoid travel during Ramadan peak hours

name: Goa, India
description: India's smallest state and beach paradise, blending Portuguese heritage with tropical coastlines, vibrant nightlife, and seafood culture.
transportation: Rented scooters/bikes, taxis, auto-rickshaws, local buses, boat cruises
safetyInfo: Generally safe. Exercise caution on beaches at night. Wear helmets when renting bikes. Watch for rip currents at some beaches.
travelTips: Rent a scooter for flexible exploration
Visit North Goa for nightlife, South Goa for serenity
Book beach shacks early during peak season
Carry cash — smaller vendors may not accept cards
Best season: November to February

name: Bali, Indonesia
description: The Island of the Gods — Bali enchants with its lush terraced rice paddies, ancient Hindu temples, world-class surf, and spiritual wellness culture.
transportation: Rented scooters, private drivers (Gojek), taxis, shuttle buses between areas
safetyInfo: Generally safe. Respect temple dress codes (wear a sarong). Be cautious on scooters. Do not touch offerings on the ground. Stay alert for petty theft.
travelTips: Hire a local driver for day trips
Book sunset spots (Tanah Lot, Uluwatu) in advance
Exchange money at authorised money changers
Dress respectfully when visiting temples
Best time: April–October (dry season)

name: Tokyo, Japan
description: A neon-lit megacity where ultra-modern skyscrapers blend seamlessly with ancient shrines, world-leading cuisine, and unmatched pop culture.
transportation: Subway (Tokyo Metro, Toei), JR trains, buses, taxis — get a Suica or Pasmo IC card
safetyInfo: One of the safest cities in the world. Very low crime. Follow etiquette (quiet on trains, queue patiently). Earthquakes are possible — know exit routes.
photographySpots: Shinjuku skyline from Tokyo Metropolitan Government Building
travelTips: Buy a 7/14-day JR Pass for unlimited train travel
Cash is still king in many places
Respect the no-eating-while-walking norm
Download Google Translate with Japanese offline pack
Best times: March–May (cherry blossom) or October–November (autumn foliage)

name: Kashmir, India
description: Heaven on Earth — Kashmir dazzles with snow-capped Himalayan peaks, shimmering lakes, Mughal gardens, and aromatic saffron valleys.
transportation: Taxis, auto-rickshaws, Shikara boats on Dal Lake, state bus services, local cabs for hill stations
safetyInfo: Check travel advisories before visiting. Stay informed of local conditions. Avoid restricted zones. Follow guidelines from your hotel and local authorities.
photographySpots: Dal Lake at sunrise from a Shikara
travelTips: Book houseboats on Dal Lake for a unique stay
Carry warm clothing even in summer for hill stations
Buy saffron and Pashmina from government-certified shops
Hire a local guide for Gulmarg and Sonamarg treks
Best season: April–June and September–October

name: Rajasthan, India
description: The Land of Kings — Rajasthan captivates with its magnificent forts, opulent palaces, vast Thar Desert, and vivid festivals.
transportation: State buses, private taxis, auto-rickshaws, camel carts in Jaisalmer, trains between cities
safetyInfo: Generally safe for tourists. Bargain firmly at markets. Watch for touts near popular forts. Drink only bottled water. Carry sunscreen — the desert sun is intense.
travelTips: Book Palace-on-Wheels train for a royal experience
Visit during October–March for best weather
Bargain at local bazaars — it is expected
Carry a scarf for sun protection and temple entry
Hire a cycle-rickshaw in Old Jaipur for a local feel

name: Maldives
description: An archipelago of 1,200 coral islands scattered across the Indian Ocean, famous for overwater bungalows, crystal-clear lagoons, and spectacular marine life.
transportation: Speedboats, domestic flights between atolls, seaplanes to remote resorts
safetyInfo: Very safe. Respect Islamic customs on local islands (dress modestly away from resorts). Alcohol only available at licensed resorts. Marine life — do not touch coral.
travelTips: Book seaplane transfers in advance — limited slots
Stay a mix of resort and local island to save costs
Bring reef-safe sunscreen
Peak season December–April (dry season)
Snorkelling gear is usually provided by resorts

name: London, United Kingdom
description: A world capital steeped in royal history, iconic red buses, incredible museums, world-class theatre, and a gloriously diverse food scene.
transportation: The Tube (Underground), Overground, Buses, Elizabeth Line, black cabs, Santander Cycles — use an Oyster card or contactless
safetyInfo: Generally very safe. Watch for pickpockets on the Tube and in crowded areas like Oxford Street. Crossing the road — remember traffic comes from the right.
photographySpots: St Paul's from the Millennium Bridge
travelTips: Use contactless card or Oyster for all transport — avoid cash on Tube
Most major museums are free
Book popular shows (West End) weeks in advance
The Tube is fast but buses let you see the city
Best weather: May–September

name: Singapore
description: A futuristic garden city-state that seamlessly blends dazzling architecture, multicultural heritage, world-class street food, and lush green spaces.
transportation: MRT (Mass Rapid Transit), buses, taxis, Grab ride-hailing — use EZ-Link card
safetyInfo: One of the safest cities in Asia. Strict laws — no littering, no chewing gum, no jaywalking. Penalties are enforced. Very clean and well-organised.
photographySpots: Gardens by the Bay Supertree Grove at night
Marina Bay Sands infinity pool view
travelTips: Get an EZ-Link card for seamless MRT and bus travel
Eat at hawker centres for authentic, affordable food
Book Gardens by the Bay light show timings
Singapore is compact — walkable between many attractions
Best time: February–April (least rain)
